text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in a quaint little town nestled among whispering oaks, nestled deep enough that rain seldom trickled down. There lived Lily featherlock, an imaginative twelve-year-old with curls as wild as the storms that rolled over the mountains.

Lily dreamed of far-off lands. One foggy evening, while her grandmother spun wool beside the fireplace, Lily imagined herself sailing on clouds. She sailed through cherry blossoms and past snowy peaks until she found herself at the edge of a moonscape. There, creatures as strange as thoughts lived.

A moody unicorn pawed curiously. A wise old owl hooted nightly tales. A gentle dragon breathed flames that melted ice. Together, they crafted spells. First, for brighter rays. Then, for speedy brooms. At last, a spell to turn summers into winters Wonderlandishly short.

Winter came early that winter night. Snow drifted like velvet. Lily found sticks for a snowy castle. In moonlight, she whispered.
